meaning: n.脊椎动物adj.有椎骨的,有脊椎的,脊椎动物
examples: The left side of most vertebrate brains, for example, seems to process and control feeding.
Thus the anaerobic energy reserves of a vertebrate are proportional to the size of the animal.
This is not surprising because the design of any flying vertebrate is subject to aerodynamic constraints.
Eons ago these messages were delivered by the genes of our vertebrate ancestors on this planet.
If the nethrops is ever to acquire some vertebrate friends, it should be tried in the Clyde too.
